title: Forge-the-witness and completed-subset still invert the GO test offline
description: MetaCoding-96q is only PARTIALLY closed. Blocked by construction: naive surgical forgery (fixture value vs witness), naive subset (orphaned witnesses), self-marking (evidence_class re-derived from the flow rather than believed), and the public oracle-seal verb is removed. STILL OPEN, measured by a fresh adversary end-to-end: (a2) edit then[i].value AND the cited observation's 'observed' field, then call seal_recording() — FULL RE-INVERSION of the GO test (wrong port 4/4=100% EXIT=0, right port 3/4), header prints '0 INVALID EVIDENCE', no warning. Cost is TWO FIELD EDITS AND A LIBRARY CALL, all offline: observation_refs key on obs_id which does not change, the orphan set does not change, and seal_recording recomputes both digests and the seal for the attacker. The implementation report claimed a forger must edit fixture+witness+refs+orphans+digests+seal; that was overstated. (b2) subset completed by pruning the orphaned witnesses: hardening-pack 9 of 11 fixtures loads CLEAN with nothing saying it is partial; on the GO pack both ports then score 2/2=100% and the wrong port becomes indistinguishable from the right one. PACKS.jsonl catches this where a ledger exists, but registry_for() returns None outside a tree containing one, so a forger works in /tmp. DIRECTION (see docs/design/iteration-methodology.md next red): a stronger hash buys nothing — an unkeyed digest cannot tell a recording from a simulation. Anchor evidence to the reviewed repository history instead.

title: oracle-seal is a public verb that re-issues a pack's entire authority — and the stated mitigation is dead code
description: The pen was RELOCATED, not removed. Invariant 2 correctly stripped the port manifest (fixture_marks now extra_forbidden, --marks gone) — then shipped an unauthenticated public verb that re-issues the pack's authority. Three executions all reach EXIT=0 / clean=true / 100%: (a) subset + re-seal, with nothing in the artifact saying the pack was partial (fresh pack_id, no lineage to the original); (b) surgical forgery — edit the expected value, recompute SemanticFixture.content_id(), re-seal — which FULLY INVERTS THE GO TEST (Port B 14/14 clean EXIT=0, Port A 10/14 EXIT=1) reported as '0 INVALID EVIDENCE, NO VERDICT 0' with no warning; (c) C2-prime — mark only the failing fixture corroboration-only in the pack + re-seal, EXIT=0 clean while '[FAIL] group_member(A) expected == True, got False' still prints in the body. Root cause: _fixture_problems (pack.py:207-250) checks only that observation_refs RESOLVE, never what they SAY, so the untouched witness file still records the source answering True. pack.py's docstring says the residual gap is 'closed socially, by REGISTRY_NAME' — grep -rn registered_seals returns exactly one hit, its own definition at pack.py:194. Zero callers; load_pack never reads PACKS.jsonl; registry_for() returns None outside a tree containing it, so an attacker simply works in /tmp. FIX DIRECTION: cross-check every expected value against the observation it cites (the witness is already in the pack and already untouched by these attacks), and make seal issuance require something the port author does not hold.
notes: PARTIALLY closed 2026-07-21. Blocked BY CONSTRUCTION: naive surgical forgery (witness now carries the observed VALUE, bound to its assertion inside the fixture hash — 'an artifact cannot endorse a claim its own witness contradicts'), naive subset (orphaned-witness rule, 'a pack is judged whole'), self-marking (evidence_class is re-derived from the flow, not believed), and the public 'ctkr oracle-seal' verb is REMOVED — a seal is issued only by seal_recording() from a recording session. registered_seals() is wired rather than deleted. Escalations still open -> MetaCoding-fmw (P0).

title: A port launders its own failures via its own manifest marks
description: Broken port: passed 25/failed 5/83.3%/EXIT=1. Add 5 corroboration_only marks to the port's OWN port.manifest.json: scored 18 (12 excluded), failed 0, 100.0%, clean=true, needs_review=[], EXIT=0. port_verify.py:540 merges port-authored and external marks with equal authority. The external path was hardened earlier today; the manifest path was not. Marks need only free text and do not block clean, while divergences need a resolvable decision_id and do. Fix: take the marks pen away from the defendant — excluded fixtures must block clean and appear in the counts.
close_reason: C2 closed AT THE MANIFEST: port.manifest.json fixture_marks is now extra_forbidden and --marks/--decisions are gone from the CLI — the pen is absent, not merely ignored (attack returns EXIT=2, pydantic extra_forbidden). BUT the same capability was RELOCATED to the pack and is fully reachable via the public oracle-seal verb; re-filed as MetaCoding-96q (P0), which is the C2 symptom verbatim one file over.

title: Probes must declare boundary vs derived — the judge's ground truth is our adapter, not farmOS
description: THE DEEPEST FINDING (wave1-readiness-v2 C1). group_member is not a value farmOS delivers: live GET /api/asset/animal has no group relationship. It is computed by a hand-written query in farmos_adapter.py:365 whose comment claims it implements 'farmOS's group-membership semantics'. It does not. farmOS's own GroupMembership.php: getGroupMembers(array $groups, bool $recurse = TRUE, $timestamp = NULL) — recursion defaults TRUE and there is a time gate; our query has neither (independently confirmed). Consequence: a port that MATCHES farmOS fails (95.2%, NOT A CLEAN PASS) while a port matching our adapter passes (100%). The acceptance direction is inverted for every derived probe. Fix: declare each probe boundary (farmOS states the value) vs derived (we compute it); derived is NON-SCORING until validated against farmOS's own authority; fix group_member recursion + time gate. This also taints two decisions bound today — membership-model (bound on a fixture our adapter produced) and movement-as-log-taxonomy (a live probe shows a movement IS an activity log and DOES inflate logCount('activity'), contradicting 1a).
close_reason: C1 CLOSED — the GO test inverted back. probes.py now declares authority (boundary vs derived) with validated_against citing the source's own authority; group_member walks the transitive closure with an effective-time gate and cycle termination, matching farmOS getGroupMembers(recurse=TRUE, timestamp). Measured on a minimal pair (diff of the two bridges = only resolveMember's body) over a 14-assertion sealed pack recorded live: farmOS-matching port 14/14 = 100% clean EXIT=0; adapter-matching port 10/14 = 71.4% not-clean EXIT=1. Pre-refactor this was inverted (95.2% not-clean vs 100% clean). Ground truth read from GroupMembership.php and re-executed via drush php:eval — 14/14 independent match, not merely self-consistent with our adapter. CAVEAT tracked as MetaCoding-ck2: no SHIPPED pack can discriminate the fix from its regression.

title: No shipped pack can discriminate the C1 fix from its regression
description: The corrected group_member derivation lives in probes.py/farmos_adapter.py, but the repo ships no recorded evidence that would catch it being re-broken. core-pack carries ONE group_member assertion (direct A-in-G) and hardening-pack two (reassignment latest-wins); none is NESTED, and none exercises the effective-time gate. Measured: on core-pack both the farmOS-matching and adapter-matching ports score 1/1 = 100%; on hardening-pack both score 2/2 = 100% — byte-identical verdicts. All the discriminating power in the GO test came from a 7-flow pack the judge authored for the occasion. Whoever re-records tomorrow against a re-broken adapter gets a green core-pack. This is C1 recursing: the fix is in the code and not in the evidence. Fix: promote the GO-test pack (transitive + effective-time cases) into the shipped packs so the regression is caught by the suite.

title: decision_covers inverts on an unresolvable decision id — a FABRICATED warrant is treated more leniently than a real one
description: port_verify.py:586 reads 'if did in decisions and not decision_covers(...)', so an id that resolves gets the topical relevance check while an INVENTED id skips it entirely. Measured: wrong answers declared with a fabricated decision_id score DIVERGED not FAILED — passed 10, diverged 4, failed 0, reproduced 10/10 = 100.0% — tying the correct port on the headline number. It does not reach clean (EXIT=3), so it is contained, but it defeats any consumer that reads the percentage rather than the exit code. The polarity is backwards: an unresolvable id should be the STRICTEST case, not the most permissive.

title: The judge waits on a deadline the defendant writes
description: C5 is only half fixed. A hanging bridge is caught in 5s when the port declares timeout: 5.0 — but the same bridge with '"timeout": 86400.0' in its own manifest produced EXIT=124 after 45s, no verdict, killed by an external timeout: exactly the original failure. BridgeSpec.timeout (port_contract.py:119) is a plain float = 30.0 with no upper bound and no judge-side cap. The judge's patience is a parameter written by the party being judged. Fix: cap it judge-side; the port may ask for less, never more.

title: The judge hangs forever on an unresponsive bridge
description: port_adapter.py:121 proc.stdout.readline() has no deadline; BridgeSpec.timeout is used only in proc.wait() at shutdown. A bridge that answers describe then sleeps: port-verify exit 124 after the external timeout, no verdict ever. At 10-15 way fan-out that is an agent that never reports rather than one that reports failure. Fix: deadline every bridge read and mandate id correlation.

title: Nothing binds a pack to its observations or a port to its pack
description: Forged fixture (value 3.0 -> 999.0, fixture_id untouched): oracle-validate says 0 errors / all valid; port-verify judges and scores it; only live oracle-verify catches it. fixtures.py load_fixtures() does model_validate_json only — content_id() is computed on write and never checked on read, and observation_refs is referenced nowhere in fixtures.py/port_verify.py/runner.py. Also: sed -n '4,7p' of a pack (the fixtures a port gets right) -> coverage 10/10, EXIT=0, clean. Fix: verify fixture_id == content_id() and resolve observation_refs at load; require a whole declared pack.
close_reason: Closed by construction for the naive case: double content addressing (whole-file seal + per-fixture content_id) catches a tampered pack — EXIT=2 'does not match its seal' and EXIT=3 'fixture_id does not hash its own body'. The surgical case (recompute the id, re-seal) survives and is tracked as MetaCoding-96q; the missing piece there is cross-checking a fixture's expected value against the observation it cites.

title: Shipped packs carry no evidence_class, so the pack-authority defence is inert
description: jq over w0a-observe/fixtures.jsonl: 12 NONE. The packs were recorded before in-pack order-sensitivity stamping shipped, so default port-verify scores the order-sensitive fixture the team ruled must never score; protection depends on remembering an optional --marks flag. port_verify.py:529 correctly refuses to let a later mark re-admit a pack-marked fixture and never fires because nothing is pack-marked. Fix: re-record w0a/w0b (verified byte-identical fixture_ids and values, ~10 min).
close_reason: Closed by the trust-model refactor: packs are content-addressed and sealed, and the shipped packs were re-recorded against the corrected adapter, so evidence class now travels with the pack rather than an optional side file. (The seal's authority being publicly re-issuable is tracked separately as MetaCoding-96q.)

title: ctkr port-verify: mechanical JUDGE + probe-surface contract
description: Largest wave-scale cost driver. There is no tool that replays an observed fixture pack against a built port — this session needed three hand-written TypeScript sandbox harnesses to judge ONE build. Needs (a) ctkr port-verify replaying a fixture pack against a port adapter, and (b) a probe-surface contract binding the fixture vocabulary (stock_on_hand, adjustment_count, log_status, has_parent, ...) to required adapter methods, so unanswerable assertions are a declared gap rather than a silent inflation of the score (13 of 30 assertions were unanswerable and the raw 24/30 overstated the build).
notes: Shipped + hardened 2026-07-20. ctkr port-verify replays an observed pack against a built port; probe-surface contract (PROBE_CONTRACT 15 assertion terms + OPERATION_CONTRACT 10 action terms) binds fixture vocabulary to required adapter methods. Generality PROVEN: an independent from-scratch Python port (different language, different layout) verified against the untouched pack with zero verifier changes, 30/30 coverage. Adversarial pass broke it twice (liar port + 30 unbacked divergences -> 100% clean exit 0; all-marks file -> zero failures exit 0); both fixed and now regression tests: decision_id required+resolved, divergences out of the numerator and blocking clean, scored_nothing flag, external marks validated, per-call unanswerable channel (the missing channel made FABRICATING a value the only unpunished move). w0a re-judged 16/16 reproduced, 13/30 unanswerable — now matches the hand harnesses exactly. 571 py / 472 TS green. REMAINING (not blocking B2's purpose): the glossary is the next bottleneck — a NEW assertion term is a code change in 8-9 files and PROBE_CONTRACT could generate that; plus an unbounded bridge read timeout (BridgeSpec.timeout used only in stop()).
close_reason: Shipped + hardened + re-attacked. The B2 report said not to close on the original numbers because two CRITICAL holes let a wrong port score green: an unbacked divergence declaration (liar port -> 100%, clean, exit 0) and a --marks file erasing every failure (exit 0). Both fixed and regression-tested: decision_id required+resolved against a registry, divergences out of the numerator and blocking clean, scored_nothing ('this run is evidence of nothing'), external marks validated, per-call unanswerable channel so fabricating a value is no longer the only unpunished move. Re-ran all attacks: exit 1 / exit 2 / exit 3 respectively. w0a re-judged 16/16 reproduced with 13/30 unanswerable — now matching the hand harnesses exactly. Remaining item (a new glossary term is a 9-file change) split to MetaCoding-yph; it is a throughput concern, not a correctness hole.

title: Re-bind the three falsified kernel decisions against observed evidence
description: Observation falsified w0b-1 (parentage-is-LWW: the correcting PATCH is accepted but INERT on lineage; an existing parent is a hard veto on the birth hook; and correct_birth is asymmetric — corrected TIME propagates, mother does not), w0b-2 (nicknames are ordered + duplicate-preserving but restatement is WHOLESALE replace, so the v1.1 GSet primitive is the wrong shape), and kernel v1.2's blanket confirmed-only pending rule (right for inventory, wrong for birth lineage/birth_date — the gate is per-projection, not global). Plus: birth-uniqueness has no oracle counterpart (farmOS 422s the second claim rather than resolving it). Needs Duke's elicitation pass with the observed evidence in hand. Structural lesson: every bound decision so far came from source reading + elicitation, never observation; treat the rest as unvalidated until observed. Evidence: eval/ctkr/results/wave1-readiness-2026-07-20.md 2.1-2.5.
close_reason: Re-bound on evidence as kernel v1.3. w0b-1 parentage-via-birth -> GuardedFirstWrite (correcting PATCH observed inert on lineage; existing parent is a hard veto), w0b-1b set_parents -> LwwRegister wholesale replace, w0b-1c birth-time-via-correction -> LwwRegister, w0b-2 nicknames -> LwwRegister<readonly V[]> with GSet now UNBOUND, pending gates -> PER-PROJECTION STATUS_CONTRACT (currentInventory/pendingInventory/adjustmentCount/parentage/birthDate rows added), w0a-2 HLC stands with its fixture marked corroboration-only, 5a birth-uniqueness stands as a FORCED divergence (farmOS 422s; the port has no coordination layer). The one CHOSEN divergence survived: yieldTotal/logCount confirmed-only. Lessons recorded: a VERB has a convergence rule, not a field; the status gate is per-projection. kernel 61/61, 475 TS, 571 py.

title: Kernel v1.2: status split — official numerics confirmed-only, pending surfaced in partner projections
description: Duke's second-pass elicitation review (2026-07-20) superseded pending-status-gates. farmOS observably counts pending harvests in yield_total/log_count (fixtures 73ed7c69, d8607818); the port deliberately diverges because a pending row in the official total makes the pending state meaningless. Implemented: new pending-only StatusGate (exact mirror of require-confirmed), STATUS_CONTRACT rows yieldTotal/logCount -> require-confirmed plus pendingYieldTotal/pendingLogCount -> pending-only, PENDING_PARTNER map with a test asserting the pair partitions candidates and that every require-confirmed numeric declares a partner. Store gained pendingYieldTotal/pendingLogCount. Observed fixtures NOT rewritten — they remain records of the source; the divergence lives in the bound decision (menuChoice supersede-with-port-semantics).
close_reason: Shipped: kernel v1.2 status split (pending-only gate, PENDING_PARTNER, store partner projections), plus all five CM decisions bound. 434/434 TS (up 4 new status tests) + 5/5 prevention green.

title: Oracle reliability + no-oracle fallback policy for the fan-out
description: Wave-1 blocker #3: farmOS Docker oracle went DOWN mid-pilot (OrbStack control plane unresponsive; docker ps hard-hangs) — observation blocked, no acceptance signal possible. Needed: (a) Duke restarts OrbStack/containers (morning); (b) oracle health-check preflight in the recipe (fail fast, not 29s hangs); (c) a WRITTEN no-oracle fallback policy: unobserved mined candidates are clearly flagged source-derived, builds judged kernel-conformance-only, value-equivalence deferred until oracle returns — never intuition-valued (the pilot already modeled this correctly); (d) consider a second oracle instance or scheduled snapshot/restore for wave-scale throughput. Overlaps bead 9h5.19 (no-live-oracle regime) — that experiment now has an unplanned real-world data point.
notes: 2026-07-20 status: (a) DONE — oracle rebuilt after OrbStack returned zero containers; live at localhost:8095, self-verified 7/7. (b) DONE — ctkr/oracle/health.py preflight on oracle-record/oracle-verify (GET /api then POST /oauth/token, 5s default timeout, exit 2 with remedy). Dead oracle now fails in 0.28s vs ~29s hang; FarmOSClient transport given an explicit timeout (root cause). The token probe also catches container-up-but-site-not-installed, which would otherwise record WRONG values. 7 hermetic tests. (c) DONE — docs/design/no-oracle-fallback.md: degrade the CLAIM, never invent the value; three prohibitions; mandatory 7/7 after rebuild; the no-intuition rule also ships inside the OracleDown message. (d) OPEN — wave-scale throughput: one instance is a single point of failure and a serialization point; options are a second instance or post-install snapshot/restore, neither evaluated. Bead stays OPEN for (d) only.
close_reason: Oracle reliability shipped. (a) Oracle rebuilt after OrbStack returned zero containers — live at localhost:8095, self-verified 7/7 core fixtures = behaviorally identical to the instance that recorded the pack; one-command ctkr/ctkr/oracle/bring-up.sh added. (b) Preflight in ctkr/oracle/health.py on oracle-record/oracle-verify: GET /api then POST /oauth/token, 5s default timeout, exit 2 with remedy — dead oracle fails in 0.28s vs the ~29s hang; root cause (FarmOSClient inheriting urllib's no-timeout default) fixed; the token probe also catches container-up-but-site-not-installed, which would have recorded WRONG values. 7 hermetic tests. (c) docs/design/no-oracle-fallback.md written — degrade the CLAIM, never invent the value; the no-intuition rule ships inside the OracleDown message. (d) SPLIT OUT to MetaCoding-d6j (wave-scale throughput: second instance or snapshot/restore) — not gating, decide when wave-1 observation volume makes serialization real. 496 py / 434 TS tests green.

title: Punt-promotion + decision extraction + agent interrupt protocol
description: Duke 2026-07-20, from the HLC unanimous-punt observation (7/7 builds independently deferred the cross-replica ordering — the kernel requirement EMERGED from deferral patterns). Three mechanisms on the ctkr decisions registry: (1) EXTRACT: 'ctkr decisions extract <build-dir>' collector that mines PORT_DECISIONS.md/ledgers from completed or in-flight builds into the registry (done manually in the 9h5.16 audit — automate it); (2) PUNT-PROMOTION: decisions with status deferred-with-dependency accumulate per topic; N punts on the same topic auto-promotes it to a kernel candidate in the elicitation menu (second-order system reading first-order declared inabilities — the cybernetic layer Duke asked for, observed working in the wild); (3) INTERRUPT: orchestrator watches the registry; when a pending shared decision blocks in-flight work per the feature x event-kind graph, message the affected agent to checkpoint+pause instead of rolling forward on a guess, then extract its decisions.

title: Feature x event-kind dependency graph: kernel-surface detector + wave scheduler

Every 9h5.16 conflict is an edge in this graph (group-assignment events folded by logs.groupMember AND location.assetsAtLocation). Uses: (1) shared kernel = high-degree event kinds, mechanically identified instead of judged; (2) wave scheduling — features sharing no kinds parallelize; features sharing kinds serialize through one expert builder or block on frozen kernel elements; (3) source-side complement is the mining read-trace (L3). Render with the existing graph tooling.
close_reason: Shipped+merged: ctkr feature-kinds (deterministic store.ts extraction + terra prose fallback, 13 tests). Real 2-feature graph: 7 kinds/17 edges; kernel surface = asset_created + log_status_changed (degree>=2), confirming the 9h5.16 status/asset-creation conflicts; group/archive/movement-vs-log honestly latent at n=2 (flagged as taxonomy tensions, not silently missed). Projected wave structure: with kernel unfrozen everything serializes through the identity/status kinds — domain lanes only parallelize AFTER kernel freeze, which independently re-derives the kernel-first mandate. Report eval/ctkr/results/feature-kind-graph-2026-07-20.md.

title: Two-feature composition run: shared store, conflicting decisions
description: Second-opinion R1/experiment 3 — the smallest honest model of the fan-out. All experiments built ISOLATED stores; the real target is one event log / one asset model / one ID scheme with overlapping projections. Compose logs+quantities and location+movement into one shared store (packs exist for both); measure cross-feature fixture pass and COUNT conflicting design decisions. title: Design-decision elicitation flows: surface, discuss, decide-for-me, or roll forward
description: Duke 2026-07-19, direct product direction from the first human CM review. When the target substrate differs from the source (which is every port), major design decisions should be SURFACED BEFORE build, not best-guessed and rolled forward — post-hoc changes cost a refactor cascade because these decisions ripple. Evidence this is real: the 9h5.4/9h5.8 ablations showed the CM-hard birth-log pick is pure builder judgment — same inputs, different builders chose preserve-via-convergence-rule vs weaken-to-eventual. The pipeline already produces the raw material (intent-CM decision menus, paradigm-divergence declarations from the meta-structural pass, port-decision records). Build the interaction layer: (1) a MENU of pending design decisions per feature, ranked by uncertainty x blast-radius (how far the decision ripples in the graph); (2) per decision, selectable flows: open an interview/elicitation discussion, 'decide for me' (agent picks + records rationale in the PD ledger), 'surface strong recommendations only', or 'roll forward' (explicit, logged, reversible-flagged); (3) decisions taken pre-build become pre-registered constraints the builder receives — connecting directly into the meta-structural pass's pre-registration discipline. This is the developer's primary role in the loop as agents handle implementation. Consider bd human as the persistence substrate for pending decisions.

title: scip-php emits no Read/Write access roles -> PHP READS_FIELD/WRITES_FIELD structurally impossible
description:
Occurrence-role histogram over the farmOS full-site scip-php index (farmos-fullsite.index.scip, bead i00): documents=726, totalOccurrences=18043, definitionOccurrences=5254, referenceOccurrences=12789, readAccessRoles=0, writeAccessRoles=0, drupalReferenceOccurrences=11309, localSymbolOccurrences=0.

FINDING: scip-php sets NEITHER SymbolRole.ReadAccess NOR SymbolRole.WriteAccess on ANY occurrence (0/18043). The loader's field-access branch (src/scip/loader.ts ~L348-354: targetKind=='field' && isWrite -> WRITES_FIELD; && isRead -> READS_FIELD) can therefore NEVER fire for PHP. Every PHP field access collapses to a plain REFERENCES edge. This is a scip-php output limitation, NOT a loader bug — scip-typescript/scip-python do set these roles.

IMPACT: PHP hom-profiles lose the read/write-field dimension. CALLS/REFERENCES/CONSTRUCTS are unaffected (CALLS is derived from callable target kind, not access role; verified 1933 CALLS + 906 CONSTRUCTS on this index).

SECONDARY: externalRefsSkipped=3903 are non-Drupal externals (symfony / PHP-stdlib) skipped by the Drupal\ gate by design (bead i00). drupalReferenceOccurrences=11309 vs kept boundary edges 4374 — the gap is refs to Drupal symbols that ARE defined in-index (resolved to real nodes, not boundary) plus refs whose enclosing caller could not be attributed.

OPTIONS: (a) accept the limitation, document PHP field-access as REFERENCES-only; (b) LSP overlay to recover read/write intent (heavy); (c) upstream scip-php PR to emit access roles; (d) tree-sitter PHP lane heuristic for $this->field = ... assignments to synthesize WRITES_FIELD.

Histogram reproducible: bun run scratchpad/repro/measure-i00.ts <index.scip> <psr4.json>.
notes:
Duke 2026-07-19: the data edges ARE the point — 'a lot of our actual benefit is through having those edges; proceeding without them feels like flying blind.' Pursue option (d) tree-sitter PHP heuristic lane for READS_FIELD/WRITES_FIELD now (immediate), and scope option (c) upstream scip-php PR (durable). Part of foundation-hardening epic 9h5.
Claude Fable 5 2026-07-19 (option d SHIPPED + option c SCOPED):

OPTION (d) DELIVERED — tree-sitter PHP field-access heuristic lane (src/extractor/edges.ts walkPhp). Synthesizes READS_FIELD/WRITES_FIELD from: $this->field / $obj->prop assignments (WRITES) + reads (READS), augmented assign ($this->count += 1) = both, Drupal ->set('f',…)/->get('f') accessors (string-keyed → external boundary node), and $entity->field_* idioms (field_ prefix → external boundary). All such edges carry a NEW provenance='tree_sitter_heuristic' attribute (Edge.provenance, new STRING column on READS_FIELD/WRITES_FIELD REL tables, additive ALTER — migrates existing DBs). SCIP-loader field edges now stamped provenance='scip' so downstream can tell them apart. farmOS validation (sandbox tree-sitter index of web/profiles/farm, 789 files): READS_FIELD 0→1089 (560 to declared props, 529 to external Drupal-field boundary nodes), WRITES_FIELD 0→252 (112 declared, 140 external). 1341 net-new data-flow edges, 100% provenance=tree_sitter_heuristic. Data-edge alphabet coverage for the farmOS PHP lane {READS_FIELD,WRITES_FIELD,TYPE_OF,RETURNS_TYPE,CONSTRUCTS} went 0/5 → 2/5; scip_fraction of PHP data edges stays 0.0 (all heuristic, correctly marked — that IS the point). Tests: 10 new PHP field-access cases in edges.test.ts (each idiom + negative local-var case) + 1 provenance round-trip in walker.test.ts; full suite 323 pass / 0 fail.

OPTION (c) UPSTREAM SCOPE — scip-php (davidrjenni/scip-php, PHP tool, cloned dev-main). ROOT CAUSE is narrow and fixable: src/DocIndexer.php. The ref() emitter (line 255) ALREADY takes an $role param (default SymbolRole::UnspecifiedSymbolRole) but the PropertyFetch branch (lines 175-187) calls $this->ref($pos,$symbol,$n->name) with NO role — so every property occurrence gets role 0. That is exactly why readAccessRoles=0/writeAccessRoles=0 over all 18043 occurrences. SymbolRole constants exist (Bindings/Scip/SymbolRole.php: WriteAccess=4, ReadAccess=8). The parser already runs nikic ParentConnectingVisitor (Parser/Parser.php:29) so every node has a 'parent' attribute — write-context detection is tractable with no new traversal. MINIMAL PR: in the PropertyFetch/NullsafePropertyFetch/StaticPropertyFetch branch, compute role from parent: $parent instanceof Assign/AssignOp/AssignRef && $parent->var === $n (also PreInc/PostInc/PreDec/PostDec, and by-ref Param/ArrayItem for completeness) → WriteAccess, else ReadAccess; pass it as ref()'s 5th arg. ~10-15 lines + imports of PhpParser\Node\Expr\{Assign,AssignOp,AssignRef,PreInc,PostInc,PreDec,PostDec}. Test fixtures live in scip-php's tests/ (occurrence snapshots). Caveat: compound LHS ($this->a->b = x) marks only the outermost fetch as write and inner as read — matches SCIP semantics from scip-typescript. Once merged, MetaCoding's loader Pass 2b field-access branch (src/scip/loader.ts:350-355) fires unchanged and yields provenance='scip' field edges that would supersede/co-exist with the heuristic lane. Source clone + farmOS source extraction + measurement scripts in scratchpad (sandbox, not committed).
close_reason: Option (d) shipped and merged: tree-sitter PHP field-access heuristic lane with provenance='tree_sitter_heuristic' marking (vs 'scip'). farmOS: READS_FIELD 0->1089, WRITES_FIELD 0->252, data-edge alphabet 0/5 -> 2/5. 341 TS tests green. Option (c) upstream fix scoped in notes (DocIndexer.php ~10-15 lines) — spun out as separate bead.

title: L3 spec-labeler mislabels large subsystems (graph-embedding attractor) + labels non-reproducible
description:
extract-spec deck defect caught by the T6 verifier on MetaCoding self-index. (1) COHERENCE: the 4 largest subsystems (~86% of members) get names+intent contradicting their members — ctkr core (2039) named 'Graph Embedding Pipeline', src/mcp (781, 91% pure) named 'Graph Embedding & Walk Generation' w/ intent about DeepWalk/Word2Vec while its interface exports EdgeKind/IndexState/CodeSearchInput. Strong 'graph embedding' attractor (MetaCoding's own graph/embedding/node vocab biases the labeler). Structural sections (interfaces/roles/composition) are CORRECT; only the LLM name+intent are wrong. Small homogeneous subsystems label correctly. (2) REPRODUCIBILITY: 9/14 card NAMES changed on identical re-run (card_ids stable; haiku temp=0 still varies + 27/291 cache misses), contradicting the 'labels stable at temp=0' claim.

## Context
Parent MetaCoding-z4w. Structural extraction is sound; the NL layer fails on exactly the biggest subsystems that matter most for a re-implementation reference. Likely fixes: (a) evidence selection via role centroids not diluted sampling; (b) hierarchical labeling — label roles/sub-clusters first then summarize the subsystem FROM its role labels (grounds summary in structure); (c) stronger model than haiku for the summary pass; (d) de-bias the attractor; (e) deeper: 2039-member subsystem is too coarse — recursive partition split. Empirical 2026-07-14, verifier on the full extract-spec run ($0.42, 14 cards).
notes:
FIXED (2026-07-14). Root cause: the subsystem name+intent — the single highest-stakes label — was built from a diluted member sample + raw code slices + FTS, ignoring the interface exports and role labels the structural lane had already extracted, so haiku pattern-matched MetaCoding's graph/embedding/node vocab.

Fixes (ctkr/ctkr/spec_cards.py, extract_spec.py, graph_loader.py):
1. HIERARCHICAL labeling — the subsystem summary is now built FROM the already-labeled roles + interface provides/consumes + top composition ops + data shapes, exports-first, not a raw member dump.
2. EVIDENCE SELECTION — feed the top interface exports (what the subsystem PROVIDES) with their L3 contracts + role labels/descriptions; raw domain-vocab slices dropped from the subsystem prompt.
3. STRONGER MODEL — subsystem pass routed to claude-sonnet-4-6 (configurable via --subsystem-model / DEFAULT_SUBSYSTEM_MODEL); cheap per-element labels stay on haiku. 14 sonnet + 277 haiku calls.
4. DE-BIAS — system prompt instructs: name from what the interface exports + roles DO, and do NOT use graph/embedding/node/walk/pipeline buzzwords unless the exports support them.
5. REPRODUCIBILITY — fixed two PYTHONHASHSEED-dependent set orderings feeding the subsystem prompt (structural_ids slice; name_tokens set iteration into the fts_terms Counter) + made search_tokens deterministic (ORDER BY rank, symbol_id). Prompts now byte-identical across 4 hash seeds; warm-cache re-run = 291/291 cache hits, 0/14 name changes (was 9/14). card_ids already stable. prompt_version bumped v1->v2; docstring 'stable at temp=0' claim replaced with honest 'regenerable view over structural ground truth'.

BEFORE -> AFTER (scip self-index, mc-v3):
2039 (ctkr core)  'Graph Embedding Pipeline'          -> 'Analysis Artifact Schema & Data Models' (intent: canonical Pydantic data models; exports LineRange/PatternRow/CentralityRow/ArtifactManifest)
781  (src/mcp)     'Graph Embedding & Walk Generation' -> 'Code Analysis Tool API' (intent: 'primary MCP tool layer for code analysis'; exports EdgeKind/IndexState/CodeSearchInput/GraphNeighborsInput)
651               'Code Graph Embedding & Walk'       -> 'Code Index Store' (exports Store/LoadScipOpts)
455               'Graph Embedding & Node Vectorization' -> 'Analysis Artifact Store and Schema' (exports CtkrHandle/openCtkrArtifacts)
Small cards stayed correct: 378 'SCIP Index Loader & Parser', 70 'LSP Client & Service Wrapper', 55 'Role Equivalence Evaluation Runner'. The graph-embedding attractor is gone from all 14 cards.

Cost: $0.42 (before, all-haiku) -> ~$0.58 (after: haiku cheap labels + sonnet 14 subsystem passes), +~$0.16. Tests: pytest 198 passed/3 skipped (excl centrality); bun test 292 pass/0 fail; new prompt-determinism verified across 4 PYTHONHASHSEEDs.

Partition-split (recursive re-partition of the 2039 subsystem) intentionally NOT done — noted follow-up; the labeling fixes alone made all 4 big cards coherent.
close_reason: Closed

title: Functor search commits zero-margin ties as mappings — add margin-aware acceptance
description:
On real code the seeded-constraint-propagation commits near-random assignments within saturated structural equivalence classes. autogen<->ag2 (verbatim fork): 96% of mappings sim>=0.95, MEDIAN MARGIN 0.0000, 91% margin<0.01; byte-identical anchor classes (ConversableAgent, GroupChat, AssistantAgent) mapped to WRONG targets. Depth-2 WL did not break enough symmetry on real code (worsened by CALLS=0).

## Context
The functor is honest at the aggregate level (fidelity/cycle-consistency rank fork ~2x above unrelated pair) but the per-symbol mapping is untrustworthy. Fix: do NOT commit a mapping whose margin < threshold — emit it as 'ambiguous/unresolved' (the margin column already exists). Report ambiguity mass instead of arbitrary ties. Depends partly on MetaCoding CALLS-edges bead. Empirical finding 2026-07-14, real Orchestrators corpus.
notes:
Implemented margin-aware honesty (does NOT change the mapping — reports the truth about it):

functorSearch.ts: new config commitMinMargin (default 0 = byte-identical to prior behavior). FunctorSearchResult gains ambiguityMass (fraction of committed mappings with margin<deltaAmb; ==ambiguityRate). FunctorMapping gains isAmbiguous (margin<deltaAmb, or <commitMinMargin when the gate is armed). When commitMinMargin>0, ambiguous mappings are FLAGGED (not dropped — kept in mapping/functor_edges) and excluded from the confident coverage/fidelity accounting.

Artifacts: FunctorRow.ambiguity_mass (float, default 0.0) + FunctorEdgeRow.is_ambiguous (bool, default False) added to schema.py canonical, *_COLUMNS tuples, functorRunner COLSPEC, hand-written types.ts, codegen'd types.gen.ts. functor_id now folds commit_min_margin into the config blob (config-for-id), so gated runs are addressable distinctly. Additive columns → old artifacts round-trip.

MCP functor_between: new min_margin query param (DB-side margin>=floor filter via artifacts.functorEdges.minMargin). Result now surfaces functor.ambiguity_mass, n_ambiguous, per-row is_ambiguous, and an honest-framing _note when ambiguity_mass>=0.5 ('N% coin-flip ties — per-symbol mapping UNRELIABLE; aggregate metrics still meaningful'). Tool descriptions + describe_api input_schema updated.

Tests: functorSearch fixture test — saturated graph (8 identical twins) yields ambiguityMass>0.9 + all isAmbiguous; clean orthogonal isomorphism yields ambiguityMass==0, none flagged; commitMinMargin gate keeps rows but zeroes confident coverage. functor_between test — saturated f:sat_ez fixture surfaces ambiguity_mass=0.75/n_ambiguous=3/note, min_margin drops ties. Python roundtrip tests assert both new fields. bun test src/ctkr src/mcp = 181 pass/0 fail; pytest test_schema = 26 pass. Determinism preserved (default path byte-identical).

NOTE: this reports honesty; it does not improve the mapping. The margin collapse is intrinsic to name-blind typed-edge profiles on real code (median margin 0.0000).
close_reason: Closed

title: scip lane emits REFERENCES but ~zero typed CALLS — the most discriminating edge is missing from hom-profiles
description:
On the real Orchestrators corpus, scip-python emitted ~60k REFERENCES and ZERO typed CALLS. Confirmed earlier on MetaCoding too (marginal-entropy showed CALLS=0 in hom-profiles). scip's occurrence model routes call-sites to REFERENCES, so the most discriminating edge dimension (who-calls-whom) is absent; profiles collapse to REFERENCES/READS_FIELD/CONTAINS count-vectors that look alike across most methods.

## Context
Root cause of the functor precision problem on real code (autogen<->ag2 fork: fidelity 0.18, median margin 0.0000, anchor classes mis-mapped). Fix options: derive CALLS from scip REFERENCES whose target is an invoked function/method, or ensure the tree-sitter CALLS survive the scip merge. High leverage: affects role_equivalent, functor discovery, and every hom-profile consumer. Empirical finding 2026-07-14 on archbox Orchestrators corpus.
notes:
CALLS recovery measured on archbox (2026-07-14). Commit 9664166 derives CALLS edges from callable REFERENCES in the SCIP loader. LOCAL: CALLS 0->4299 edges on MetaCoding self-index; marginal-entropy CALLS importance NONE->HIGH (+0.3132 bits, 4th of 15 kinds). ARCHBOX autogen v0.2.40 vs ag2: CALLS 0->29278 edges (2nd largest kind). Functor autogen->ag2: fidelity 0.180->0.199 (+10.6%), coverage 0.686->0.678, cycle 0.543->0.539. Median margin STILL 0.0000 (margin collapse NOT broken; pct>0 = 24.2%). Anchor classes STILL mis-mapped: ConversableAgent->BaseEvent, GroupChat->CaseResult, AssistantAgent->Depends. VERDICT: CALLS recovery is a real signal (HIGH importance, measurable fidelity lift) but is NOT sufficient alone to break the margin collapse or fix anchor-class mapping. The bottleneck is elsewhere -- likely profile saturation from the high-dimensional hom-vector space where structurally similar but semantically different classes remain indistinguishable.
IMPLEMENTED + MEASURED (2026-07-14, commit 9664166). Fix: SCIP has no call occurrence role, so the loader now ADDS a CALLS edge when a resolved REFERENCE target is a callable (keeping REFERENCES). LOCAL (MetaCoding --scip): CALLS 0 -> 4299 edges; marginal-entropy CALLS +0.0000/NONE -> +0.2208/HIGH (4th strongest); overall entropy 4.850 -> 4.872. ARCHBOX autogen0.2.40<->ag2 fork: fidelity 0.180 -> 0.199, coverage 0.686 -> 0.678, cycle 0.543 -> 0.539 — NEGLIGIBLE. Margin collapse PERSISTS: median 0.0000, 89% <0.01 (baseline 91%). Anchor classes still mostly mis-map at zero margin. VERDICT: CALLS recovery is correct hygiene (real +0.22 role-discrimination dimension, likely helps partition/labels) but does NOT fix isomorphism precision — because derived-CALLS overlaps REFERENCES (adds little NEW info). The margin collapse is FUNDAMENTAL to name-blind typed-edge profiles on real code, not a missing-edge artifact. Real fixes: MetaCoding-265 (margin-aware acceptance = report ambiguity honestly), role-level not symbol-level gates (extraction port-verifier already does this), and for same-name FORK detection specifically a lexical tiebreaker (names ARE legit signal there; not for cross-stack ports where names don't survive).

title: Marginal-entropy measurement harness for edge kinds
description: Measure each edge kind's marginal contribution to hom-profile entropy on a --scip corpus, so edge-taxonomy work is driven by data (which edges actually move entropy toward clean GO) rather than by entropy-check's static list.
design: Leave-one-edge-kind-out (and add-one-in) over hom_profiles: recompute shannon_entropy/top5 with each EdgeKind ablated. Rank kinds by entropy delta. Reuse ctkr entropy-check plumbing. Corpus at 4.845 BORDERLINE is the baseline.
acceptance_criteria: A ranked report of per-edge-kind marginal entropy delta on the scip corpus; verdict on whether RAISES/DECORATES are worth the extractor cost before building them.

title: Add DECORATES edge kind (decorator application) to extractor
description: New typed edge: decorator symbol -> decorated symbol (Python '@dec', TS '@Decorator'). First confirm ANNOTATES doesn't already capture decorator application.
design: PREREQUISITE CHECK: inspect what ANNOTATES currently emits (types.ts lists it uncommented; may be type-annotation edges, not decorators). If ANNOTATES already covers @decorators, close this as redundant and instead document the semantics. Otherwise add DECORATES via tree-sitter (decorator syntax nodes in py/ts).
acceptance_criteria: Either: DECORATES edges emitted for decorated fixtures AND ANNOTATES-vs-DECORATES semantics documented; OR bead closed with a note that ANNOTATES already covers it.

title: Add RAISES edge kind (exception flow) to extractor
description: New typed edge: function/method -> exception/error type it raises or throws. Extends EdgeKind enum + EDGE_KIND_VALUES + schema PLAIN_EDGES.
design: Tree-sitter lane: Python 'raise X(...)' and TS/JS 'throw new X()' / 'throw X'. Map the thrown/raised type reference to the enclosing function symbol. SCIP lane: emit if the type is resolvable, else tree-sitter-only. No RAISES today (grep of edges.ts/scip loader confirms absent).
acceptance_criteria: RAISES edges emitted for a fixture containing raise/throw; entropy-check on a --scip corpus shows a populated RAISES column; hom-profile entropy delta measured vs baseline.

title: Extend edge taxonomy: RAISES + DECORATES to sharpen hom-profiles (BORDERLINE→GO)
description:
Push hom-profile entropy from BORDERLINE to a clean GO by adding the genuinely-missing typed edges, so Phase 2a role-equivalence and Phase 2b functor discovery get sharp role classes.

## Context
Empirical finding 2026-07-13: on a --scip self-index of MetaCoding, hom-profile shannon_entropy=4.845 bits (BORDERLINE, top5=67.7%, 16.4% pairs >0.9 sim), up from 3.818 (BLOCKED) with tree-sitter only. The scip CALLS/REFERENCES lift (+1.03 bits, edges 3002->8367) crossed the 4.0 gate. entropy-check still recommends richer edges for sharper roles. IMPORTANT: its hardcoded message lists READS_FIELD/WRITES_FIELD/RETURNS_TYPE/CONSTRUCTS which ALREADY EXIST in the 14-kind EdgeKind enum (added by MetaCoding-e54/3s5/9le). The TRUE gaps are RAISES (exception flow) and DECORATES (decorator application; ANNOTATES may already partially cover it). Drive further edge work by measured marginal entropy, not the static list.
notes: MEASURED OUTCOME (2026-07-13, MetaCoding --scip self-index): entropy 4.845 -> 4.850 (+0.005, NOISE). Still BORDERLINE. New edges negligible on this corpus: RAISES=6 edges (+0.004, LOW), ANNOTATES/decorators=0 edges (NONE - verify on a decorator-heavy repo). Signal is carried by REFERENCES (+1.62), CONTAINS (+1.18), READS_FIELD (+0.61). CALLS=0 in hom-profiles (scip routes call-sites through REFERENCES). Conclusion: the deliverable that paid off is the ijo.3 marginal-entropy harness, which proves exotic edge kinds have diminishing returns here and localizes the real signal. BORDERLINE->GO lever is NOT more edge kinds; likely CONTAINS-scaffolding down-weighting and/or verifying ANNOTATES fires on decorator-heavy code.

title: serve reopen-on-refresh: pick up a reindex without restart
description: Read-only serve is snapshot-pinned at startup (scripts/spike-refresh.ts: a live RO handle does NOT see another process's commits; only a full Database reopen does). So after a reindex, serve keeps serving the startup snapshot until restarted. Add a cheap reopen trigger.
design: graph.lbug is a single file. Stat its mtime; when it advances past the open-time mtime, swap in a fresh read-only Store (open new, route new calls to it, close the old once in-flight queries drain). Trigger by: (a) mtime check before each tool call (debounced), or (b) the indexer touching a generation/manifest file that serve watches. Surface the swap via the existing index-state staleness fields. Requires routing tool handlers through a getStore() accessor instead of a captured const.
acceptance_criteria: With serve running, a  completes and a subsequent describe_api/graph_callers call reflects the new graph without restarting serve.
close_reason: serve reopen-on-refresh: Store.generation() mtime marker + currentStore() accessor reopens the read-only handle when graph.lbug advances; all graph/text/describe handlers route through it; dedup + grace-delay close. smoke-reopen.ts (REOPEN_SMOKE_PASS) + full gauntlet green.

title: ladybugdb concurrency: reads during indexing
description: Spikes (scripts/spike-lock.ts, spike-refresh.ts) established ladybugdb's real lock semantics: the exclusive lock excludes only OTHER WRITERS — read-only opens coexist with a live writer on the same dir (both directions), but a read-only handle is snapshot-pinned at open time and needs a full reopen to advance. The 'easy 80%' (read-only Store.open for serve/query/status) is DONE and merged. This epic tracks the remaining freshness/coordination work.
design: Done: readOnly Store.open; serve/query/status read-only so they coexist with index. Remaining: serve reopen-on-refresh, checkpoint cadence, optional blue-green, optional single-writer daemon.
close_reason: 3/4 children done and merged; sole remainder gh0.4 (single-writer daemon) is deferred-optional and remains tracked as its own deferred bead. Duke approved closing during 2026-07-19 foundation-hardening review.

title: metacoding status CLI + serve startup empty-store warning
description: Give humans the same signal: a 'metacoding status' command, and a one-line stderr warning at serve startup when the store is empty (Claude Code surfaces MCP stderr). Also echo the resolved data dir on serve — index already prints it as JSON, serve prints nothing.
design: Add 'status' subcommand in src/cli/main.ts that resolves the data dir (cli/data-dir.ts) and prints store.summary() human-readable. In serveMcp() (mcp/server.ts:34) after Store.open, if summary.symbols===0 write to stderr: '⚠ 0 symbols at <dataDir> — run: metacoding index . --scip'. Always log the resolved dataDir to stderr on serve start.
acceptance_criteria: 'metacoding status' prints indexed/empty + counts + dataDir for the cwd repo. Starting serve on an empty store emits the warning to stderr; on an indexed store it logs the dataDir and no warning.
close_reason: metacoding status CLI (human + --json) and serve startup stderr echo + empty-store warning. Tested on indexed/empty/json.

title: store.summary(): single source of truth for index state
description: Add Store.summary() returning a cheap snapshot of what's actually in the store. The current code has no count/status method at all; every consumer reinvents emptiness checks or skips them.
design: New method on Store (src/store/index.ts). One Cypher: MATCH (n:Symbol) RETURN count(n). Plus distinct (repo, repo_commit_sha) and max(indexed_at) from the temporal columns. Return { dataDir, symbols, repos: [{repo, commit, indexedAt}], indexed: symbols>0 }. Keep it a read-only single round-trip so it's safe to call on serve startup and from describe_api.
acceptance_criteria: Store.summary() returns symbols=0 / indexed=false on a freshly-opened empty dir, and accurate counts + repo/commit list on an indexed dir. Covered by a smoke test (scripts/smoke-*.ts) exercising both the empty and indexed paths.
close_reason: Store.summary() added (pure store read: symbols, indexed, per-repo snapshots). Verified by SUMMARY_SMOKE_PASS (empty=0/false, filled>0/true).

title: Surface index state in describe_api
description: Agents are already instructed to call describe_api first. Make it report store.summary() so the agent self-detects an empty/unindexed workspace instead of trusting [] from graph_callers et al.
design: In mcp/tools.ts describeApi() (and its wiring in mcp/server.ts:184), add a 'store' block: { indexed, symbols, repos, dataDir }. No new tool, no protocol risk — just enrich the payload the agent already fetches.
acceptance_criteria: describe_api output includes an index-state block; on an unindexed repo it clearly reads indexed:false with the dataDir to run 'metacoding index . --scip' against.
close_reason: describe_api(indexState?) now emits index_state + index_state_hint (not-indexed/stale/healthy). 5 unit tests pass; smoke shows 17 tools.

title: Index-state observability: distinguish 'not indexed' from 'no results'
description: Today Store.open() (src/store/index.ts:23) mkdirSync's the data dir and seeds schema on open, so an UN-indexed workspace is indistinguishable from an indexed-but-empty one: serve starts happily and every tool returns []. An agent cannot tell 'nothing matches' from 'there is nothing here', and after the user registered the MCP at user scope, every fresh repo starts in exactly this silent-empty state. This epic adds a single source of truth for index state and surfaces it everywhere an agent or human would look: describe_api, a status CLI, serve startup, and staleness vs HEAD. Net effect: empty/stale graphs announce themselves instead of masquerading as valid answers.
design: Foundation: one store.summary() method everything else reads from. Then three surfaces (describe_api, status CLI, serve stderr) + staleness + friendly lock errors + README correction.
close_reason: Epic complete: all 6 children closed. Index state now self-announces via summary/describe_api/status/serve; staleness + lock-error UX; README fixed. Full smoke gauntlet green.

title: Glossary term addition is a 9-file code change — generate it from PROBE_CONTRACT
description: Next bottleneck after B2 (from the port-verify wave-scale review). Adding a NEW fixture assertion term currently touches 8-9 files (glossary, probes contract, adapter ABC, farmos adapter, port adapter, runner dispatch, flowspec_io validation, tests). 14 of the 25 current terms came from just two features, so wave-1's remaining features will each want new vocabulary. Also fix: BridgeSpec.timeout is used only in stop(), so PortBridge.call() does a bare blocking readline() — a hung port bridge hangs the wave; and methods_when_timed is dead code.

title: Close the semantic prevention gates the w0a build left open
description: The gates that guard against divergence at 100+ builds are open in the pilot build: the status gate is a feature-local constant instead of a STATUS_CONTRACT row (the exact re-litigation status.ts exists to forbid, and worse now that the pending gate must be per-projection), the behavioural sub-taxonomy is unregistered with a silent catch-all decrement, and the projection is not kind-guarded. Also complete the build's surface (adjustment_count per the re-opened v1.2 rule, a log-status read, the two restatement verbs) before any further conformance claim.
close_reason: Gates closed + the unfiled structural one. (1) The build VENDORED a copy of src/kernel that had already drifted to the pre-v1.3 status contract — deleted, now imports the one kernel; a copied kernel prevents nothing at wave scale. (2) Status gate was a feature-local constant -> gateFor('currentInventory') (the v1.3 row set by observation). (3) Behavioural sub-taxonomy had a silent catch-all decrement for any unknown sub-kind -> closed set, throws. (4) Projection was not kind-guarded -> guards first, proven by a REAL composed store (shared registry+log, neighbouring kind registered) and mutation-checked: without the guard it reads 1003 instead of 4. A first draft of that test called a non-existent __log hook and asserted nothing — replaced. Build 15/15 (was 11/11); port-verify 16/16 reproduced, unchanged, as expected since gates prevent divergence rather than alter values. Surface completion split to a follow-up.

title: Fixture schema admits non-reproducible and non-agnostic fixtures
description: Two schema-level gaps found by observation. (1) A relative 'at' plus any timestamp-returning probe produces a fixture that cannot self-verify — w0b first self-verified at 63.6%, every failure a uniform +24s wall-clock gap; caught only because self-verify ran twice, fixed by hand. Forbid relative at in such flows or teach the format a flow-relative expected value. (2) There is no way to mark a fixture corroboration-only / order-sensitive: w0a-2's observed 3.0 encodes farmOS insertion-id order (6 permutations yield 4 distinct values), so it scores as a false green today and a false failure under any other replica ordering.
close_reason: Shipped + proven. (1) Relative-at + timestamp-probe now REJECTED at load (ProbeSpec.returns_timestamp); verified by rebuilding the original w0b defect — rejected with reason and fix, before the oracle is touched. (2) Order-sensitivity is DETECTED, not declared: detect_order_sensitivity() finds same-effective-time writes against one subject; re-recording w0a's flow against live farmOS classified it corroboration-only unprompted, with the reason, in provenance. Evidence class travels WITH the pack (Provenance.evidence_class), precedence pack < manifest < --marks, but a pack-carried mark cannot be UNDONE: a port attempting to re-admit an unscoreable fixture gets a declaration problem. No fixture ids churned (provenance is outside the content hash). 585 py / 475 TS.

title: Recorder must record a REFUSAL instead of dying on it
description: record_session aborts on the first AdapterError, so a write the source REFUSES — the sharpest semantic signal available — kills the run instead of becoming a fixture. w0b lost 1 of 12 flows this way, and the UniqueBirthLog 422 (farmOS refuses a second birth claim rather than resolving it) became a footnote instead of an asset. Add an expect_refusal / observed-error step so refusal semantics are recordable.
close_reason: Shipped + proven live. title: Single-repo / endofunctor mode for functor discovery (diagonal exclusion + depth-as-dial)
description:
functor_between currently assumes distinct repos (C_A -> C_B). Add a single-repo mode: functor_between(R, R) with the diagonal (s->s) EXCLUDED, to surface non-trivial INTERNAL functors — isomorphic subsystems within one project (a pattern instantiated twice, parallel modules). Without diagonal exclusion the search returns the trivial identity (coverage 1.0). Also codify depth-as-a-dial in the tool docs.

## Context
From the 2026-07-13 design discussion. Cross-repo isomorphism is the native case; single-repo needs diagonal exclusion. Depth is a use-case knob: --depth 1 profiles for role/symmetry SURFACING (role_equivalent — you WANT the orbits), --depth 2 (WL refinement, commit 611ae33) for precise 1:1 correspondence (it deliberately shrinks orbits). Zero-profile ~22.7% is a shared structural-isolation floor at any depth. Directly useful for the subsystem-spec-extraction use case: verifying two subsystems within one project are isomorphic, and as the port-verifier (functor_between(S_original, S_reimplemented)). See docs/design/ct-functor-discovery.md and docs/notes/functor-spike/2hop-findings.md.

title: Checkpoint cadence + chunked write txns (reader freshness)
description: Readers only advance at checkpoints (spike-refresh). A long index that checkpoints only at the end means RO readers see nothing mid-run. Tune ladybug autoCheckpoint/checkpointThreshold (SystemConfig) and/or per-file write transactions so progress flushes incrementally and reopened readers see it sooner. Pairs with incremental AST-hash indexing.
acceptance_criteria: During a long index, a fresh read-only open sees monotonically increasing symbol counts mid-run (already observed informally: 355→370); document the chosen cadence.
close_reason: Confirmed live: readers see index progress mid-run (auto-checkpoint already incremental, 355->370). Documented the auto-checkpoint reliance + autoCheckpoint/checkpointThreshold as a future lever. No indexer refactor needed.

title: Friendly single-writer lock error
description: ladybugdb is single-writer; running index/watch while serve holds the store (or vice versa) surfaces a raw 'Could not set lock on file ... graph.lbug' internal message. This also caused the 'Failed to connect' we saw when 'claude mcp list' health-checked serve mid-index.
design: Catch the lock error at Store.open() (src/store/index.ts:22) and rethrow with an actionable message naming the likely cause: 'store is locked by a running metacoding serve/watch on this repo — stop it before indexing'. Match on ladybugdb's lock-error text/code.
acceptance_criteria: Opening a store already locked by another process yields the friendly message, not the raw ladybugdb string.
close_reason: Store.open wraps lock errors with actionable 'locked by serve/watch' message, preserving cause.

title: Staleness detection: graph vs current HEAD + dirty files
description: Emptiness is only half the trap; the worse half is a graph frozen at index time that LOOKS right after the tree moved. graph_callers silently goes stale once files change. We already ship live lsp_references as the fallback — this tells the agent WHEN to prefer it.
design: Extend store.summary() / describe_api with staleness: compare the stored repo_commit_sha against live 'git rev-parse HEAD' and count dirty files via 'git status --porcelain'. Surface { headBehind: bool, indexedCommit, currentCommit, dirtyFiles }. describe_api note: 'graph indexed at X, HEAD is Y, N dirty files — prefer lsp_references for changed files.'
acceptance_criteria: On a repo where HEAD advanced or files are dirty since indexing, describe_api/status report the drift with indexed vs current commit and dirty count; clean+current repo reports no drift.
close_reason: Staleness in gatherIndexState/describe_api/status: indexed vs live HEAD + dirty count. Live test showed head_behind + 10 dirty files.

title: ANNOTATES only fires for repo-local decorators, not imported ones
description:
ANNOTATES (decorator application, from MetaCoding-ijo.2) resolves the decorator by shortName to an in-repo function/class symbol (edges.ts tsHandleDecorator ~L505, and the Python path). Imported/framework decorators (@dataclass, @Component, @pytest.fixture, @app.route) don't resolve to a repo-local symbol, so NO edge is emitted.

## Context
Empirically ANNOTATES=0 on both MetaCoding (34 @dataclass decorators, indexed, 2078 py nodes) and aspects-webapp. Unit tests pass because they use repo-local decorators. To make ANNOTATES contribute on real code, emit edges to external/unresolved decorator symbols (as SCIP does with external refs, or a synthetic decorator node). LOW PRIORITY: per the ijo.3 marginal-entropy finding, low-count edge kinds barely move entropy, so decorators are unlikely to be worth much even if fixed. Documented so it isn't rediscovered.
close_reason: ANNOTATES now emits boundary-node edges for imported/unresolved decorators in both TS and Python paths (externalFallback), 58/58 edges tests pass; feeds A1 intention signal for T5a

title: PHP SCIP: resolve into Drupal core (full site build)
description: Current farmOS scip-php index resolves intra-farmOS refs (84%) but leaves ~797 refs into Drupal core / PHP stdlib external. For full fidelity (refs into ContentEntityBase, FormBase, etc.), build a real Drupal-11 farmOS site (farmOS docker/ dev env or composer create-project) so vendor/ has drupal/core, then run scip-php there. notes:
INVESTIGATED 2026-07-13, deferred per Duke. Findings:
DISK: built farmOS+Drupal-11 site = 244MB (vendor 40M + web 204M; core 130M/11k .php, farm 57M/772, contrib 12M). Image farmos/farmos:4.x = 1.7GB incl runtime.
FEASIBLE: Drupal\Core base classes (ContentEntityBase/FormBase/ControllerBase/PluginBase) DO resolve from the built site's vendor (class_exists passes) — these are the main functor anchors. Core MODULE classes (Drupal\node, Drupal\views) need their PSR-4 registered separately.
APPROACH: docker pull farmos/farmos:4.x; docker cp /opt/drupal to disk; swap in target farmOS as web/profiles/farm; synthesize farm-only PSR-4 autoload; dump-autoload; run scip-php from site root so Drupal\Core resolves via vendor (index farm only, NOT core internals — resolve core as boundary targets).
BLOCKERS (scip-php maturity vs modern Drupal 8.4 syntax), each individually solvable:
 1. scip-php:latest image is PHP 8.2; farmOS core 11.3 needs 8.4 (platform_check) — neutralize vendor/composer/platform_check.php.
 2. Image's bundled nikic/php-parser too old for PHP 8.4 syntax -> parse crash on contrib + 4.x-dev farm.
 3. Current scip-php (dev-main, PHP 8.5, nikic 5.7) crashes in composer/class-map-generator PhpFileParser:58 on a site file.
 4. Running scip-php inside the site: its bundled nikic collides with Drupal's own vendor/nikic -> NodeTraverser crash.
LOADER TODO for boundary edges: Pass 2b currently SKIPS external refs (externalRefsSkipped). To capture farmOS->Drupal\Core EXTENDS/IMPLEMENTS, emit edges to boundary nodes via externalQn for resolved scip-php external symbols (gate to Drupal\ targets to avoid symfony/stdlib noise).
See memory scip-php-integration.

description: Wire scip-php into src/scip/run.ts. BLOCKED on packaging: scip-php is a Go binary (Sourcegraph), not an npm package, so it doesn't fit the current npm-based resolveScipBin/PKG_FOR_BIN model. Needs: Go toolchain or vendored binary distribution, a new ScipLanguage='php', an INDEXERS entry, loader guessLanguageFromQn '.php', and CLI detectScipLanguages. Punted per user (extractor lane shipped first, mirrors how Python shipped).
notes: DONE 2026-07-13: PHP SCIP lane built + validated on farmOS source. scip-php via Docker (davidrjenni/scip-php). Loader reconciles PHP FQN symbols with tree-sitter via phpRealFile (PSR-4 real-path recovery) + synthesized enclosing scopes (scip-php emits no enclosing_range). scripts/scip-php-prep.ts automates Drupal autoload synth + composer + index + psr4 sidecar. Result: 4755/5244 scip defs merge onto tree-sitter; 1440 REFERENCES + 904 CONSTRUCTS cross-file. Commit a4e6877.
close_reason: Built and validated on farmOS; commit a4e6877. Cross-Drupal-core resolution (full site build) tracked separately.

title: Optional: single-writer daemon (serve owns ladybug)
description: Long-term: one process opens ladybug RW and owns it; index/query/status become thin clients over a unix socket. Gives concurrent reads + a serialized write queue across processes and zero-staleness reads (no reopen). Most flexible, most work; only needed once read-only+reopen is insufficient.
acceptance_criteria: All store access funnels through one owner process; no second process ever opens the DB directly.

title: Optional: blue-green staging swap + snapshot retention
description: With read-only coexistence shipped, blue-green is no longer required for availability, but still gives atomic visibility (never expose a half-built graph) and snapshot retention (free graph_diff across commits). Index into staging-<ts>/, atomic-rename to live, serve reopens via the manifest pointer.
acceptance_criteria: Reindex never exposes a partial graph to readers; N prior snapshots retained and diffable.
close_reason: Dropped: read-only coexistence (gh0 80%) + serve reopen-on-refresh (gh0.1) remove the availability need that motivated blue-green. Atomic-visibility / snapshot-retention can be revived as a fresh bead if a concrete need arises.

title: Fix README data-dir drift (./.metacoding vs XDG)
description: README repeatedly says the default data dir is ./.metacoding, but resolveDataDir() (cli/data-dir.ts:62) defaults to $XDG_DATA_HOME/metacoding/<repo-id>/ (~/.local/share/...) unless a legacy ./.metacoding already exists or --data-dir is passed. This mismatch sent us hunting for the store earlier.
design: Correct README Install/Quick-start to describe the XDG default + repo-id hashing and the legacy-./.metacoding precedence. Pairs with the serve 'echo resolved data dir' from the status bead so docs and runtime agree.
acceptance_criteria: README accurately states the default data-dir resolution order; no remaining claim that ./.metacoding is the unconditional default.
close_reason: README data-dir resolution corrected to flag>legacy ./.metacoding>XDG <repo-id>; removed false default claims.
